summary: Admin Dashboard end-to-end testing. Backend: 37/37 pytest cases passing across /admin-auth/login (success/wrong-pw/wrong-email), /admin-auth/me (Bearer + no-token), 9 admin endpoints all returning 401/403 without auth, dashboard stats shape, revenue series for day(24)/week(7)/month(30), orders search across 4 windows + q/status filters, status update validation (invalid status -> 400, missing order -> 404), CSV export with correct content-type and attachment headers, customers list + 404 detail, loom credits summary/per-user/redeemed-orders, adjust validation (zero rejected, user-not-found 404), storefront /products + /payments/config (demo mode confirmed) + guest /payments/create-order in demo mode, and an E2E test that creates an order then admin updates its status to 'packed' and verifies via search + CSV. Frontend: /admin unauth redirect -> /admin/login, login form renders, wrong creds shows error, correct creds redirect to /admin, all stat cards present (Today/Week/Month/Pending/Customers), revenue chart with 24h/7d/30d toggle, sidebar nav for Dashboard/Orders/Customers/Loom Credits works, Sign Out returns to /admin/login, storefront / loads with Navbar, admin chrome (sidebar + Sign Out) separated from storefront Navbar/Footer. CSV button + search input present on /admin/orders with filter buttons functional.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking 'Pieces' in admin sidebar navigates to /login (storefront login) instead of an admin product-editor route. Either the link points to a route that's still guarded by legacy Google-OAuth user auth (and admin JWT doesn't satisfy it), or no admin-pieces page exists. Other 4 sidebar links (Dashboard/Orders/Customers/Loom Credits) all work correctly.

Recharts emits console warning 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on first paint and on tab switching. Chart renders correctly visually — cosmetic only. Likely needs an explicit min-width/min-height on the ResponsiveContainer wrapper.

action_items: Wire the 'Pieces' sidebar link in AdminShell to a proper admin product editor route (or remove it). Currently it routes the user to /login, which is confusing for an authenticated admin.
(Optional) Add explicit min-height/min-width on Recharts ResponsiveContainer in AdminDashboardPage.jsx to suppress the 'width(-1)' console warning on first paint.
critical_code_review_comments: admin_auth.seed_admin re-hashes the password whenever the env password no longer verifies — this is convenient for recovery but means rotating ADMIN_PASSWORD in .env silently rewrites the DB on every restart; consider logging at WARNING level or gating behind an explicit flag.

/api/admin-auth/login sets cookie with secure=True, samesite='none' which is correct for cross-origin preview but will break on plain-HTTP dev servers — token is also returned in JSON body so frontend can fall back to Authorization header (good).

Lockout in admin_auth._is_locked_out is keyed by '{ip}:{email}'. If an attacker rotates IPs (or sits behind a load balancer that varies X-Forwarded-For), lockout is bypassed. Acceptable for an internal admin tool; document the limitation.

require_admin in server.py accepts EITHER the new admin_auth JWT OR a legacy Google-OAuth user with is_admin — this dual path is by design but means revoking the admin user in admin_users still allows the legacy OAuth path. Worth a comment in code.

/api/admin/dashboard/revenue does string comparison of ISO timestamps (created_at >= start.isoformat()) which works because all created_at strings are UTC ISO with the same shape. If a future code path inserts a non-UTC or naive datetime string, the filter silently misses rows. Consider normalizing on write.
/api/admin/orders.csv loads up to 5000 orders in memory and serializes to a StringIO. Fine for now, but for a larger catalog stream the CSV with StreamingResponse.

POST /payments/create-order persists the order doc and the negative-balance redeemed txn BEFORE Razorpay returns successfully. In live mode an upstream RZP failure would leave a phantom order + redeemed txn in DB. In demo mode (current setup) this is not reachable. Same comment carried from iteration_2.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (18/19 flows — Pieces sidebar link routes to /login instead of an admin route)

test_credentials: Admin: crescent.looom@gmail.com / Crescentloom@2026 (already in /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Auto-seeded on backend startup from .env.
seed_data_creation: Backend E2E test created 2 guest orders via /api/payments/create-order in demo mode (one for the demo-mode test, one for the E2E flow). Both are visible on the dashboard as ₹798 total revenue (2 x ₹399 Textured Polo Tee). These can be cleared by deleting orders where shipping.full_name starts with 'TEST_'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin Dashboard is fully functional. Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_admin_dashboard.py covers admin-auth, all admin endpoints (orders/customers/loom/dashboard/CSV), auth guard parameterized across 9 endpoints, and a create-order -> admin-update-status E2E. To re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_admin_dashboard.py -v. Razorpay is in demo mode (no RZP_KEY_ID/SECRET set), so /payments/create-order returns {demo_mode: true, razorpay_order: null}. Telegram/SMTP are fire-and-forget — they will log skip/error but never raise; do not be surprised if real Telegram messages are sent to chat -5102349304 when status updates occur. Two created test orders remain in the DB (TEST_Buyer, TEST_Workflow) for inspection.
rca of the issue: Pieces sidebar nav: link target in AdminShell.jsx likely points to a route like /admin/products or /products/new that is wrapped by require_user (cookie session_token) rather than the AdminAuthProvider — so when the user clicks, the storefront's ProtectedRoute kicks in and redirects to /login. Fix: either expose an admin-only Pieces page (CRUD over /api/admin/products) wrapped by AdminAuthProvider, or remove the sidebar entry until that page exists.
